pi_items.source: task.json id field and setup_task.sh confirmation
World Rugby Laws of the Game (official rugby union specification): https://www.dimensions.com/element/rugby-goal-post confirms 5.6 meters is the official width of rugby goal posts
task.json description: 'A try has been scored exactly 10 meters to the side of the goal post' and setup_task.sh creates geometry data
Mathematical derivation: sqrt(try_offset * (try_offset + goal_width)) = sqrt(10 * 15.6) = sqrt(156) ≈ 12.49. Export script confirms this formula at line 150.
export_result.sh line 156 uses tolerance of 0.5 meters for optimal point verification
setup_task.sh lines 34-40 create this file during task initialization with goal width value

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified. The task uses official rugby union dimensions (5.6m goal post width per World Rugby specifications) for a calculus optimization problem where a kicker must find the optimal distance to maximize the angle subtended by the goal posts. The try is positioned 10m from the goal line, and the theoretical optimum distance (where the viewing angle is maximized) is 12.49 meters, calculated via sqrt(10 × 15.6). The task creates a synthetic geometry problem within GeoGebra where the agent must model these dimensions, create an angle function graph, and identify the optimal viewing position within a tolerance of 0.5 meters.
